description: User-controlled notification preference system with per-channel overrides and a Do-Not-Disturb mode that suppresses all notifications for a configurable period with automatic expiry.

actors.name: End User
actors.description: Configures their own notification preferences and DND schedule

actors.name: DND Expiry Job
actors.description: Background process that checks and expires timed DND statuses every minute

fields.name: notification_comments
fields.label: When to notify about thread replies

fields.name: mention_keys
fields.label: Comma-separated custom keywords that trigger notifications when they appear in m

fields.name: notify_on_first_name
fields.label: Whether the user's first name is treated as a mention keyword

fields.name: channel_notification_override
fields.label: Per-channel overrides for desktop, mobile, and email notification levels; overri

fields.name: channel_mark_unread
fields.label: Per-channel mute control — whether all messages or only mentions mark the channe

fields.name: dnd_end_time
fields.label: Unix timestamp in seconds at which a timed DND status expires; zero means DND is

fields.name: prev_status
fields.label: Previous availability status stored so it can be restored when DND expires

values.name: online
values.description: User is actively using the application

values.name: dnd
values.description: Do Not Disturb — push notifications are suppressed regardless of other preferences

transitions.description: User activates DND from away state
Timed DND expires; status restored to prev_status

rules.rule_01: Notification preference resolution follows a three-level hierarchy: channel-level override → user-level default → system default.
rules.rule_02: A channel-level setting of 'default' falls back to the user's global preference for that notification type.
rules.rule_03: When DND is active, push notifications are suppressed even if the user's push preference is set to 'all'.
rules.rule_04: DND does not suppress email notifications by default; email is governed only by the email notification preference.
rules.rule_05: Direct messages apply 'all' push notification logic even if the user has 'mention' set as their general push preference.
rules.rule_06: Group messages also use 'all' push logic, treating them equivalently to direct messages.
rules.rule_07: A timed DND end_time is truncated to one-minute intervals, aligning with the expiry job's polling interval.
rules.rule_08: DND expiry checks run every minute; the status reverts to prev_status on expiry.
rules.rule_09: Custom mention keys are comma-separated; they trigger notifications identically to username @-mentions.
rules.rule_10: Thread notification preferences (desktop_threads, push_threads, email_threads) are independent of top-level preferences and apply specifically to replies in followed threads.

given: all preference values are within allowed option sets
preferences_updated.result: New preferences take effect for the next incoming notification

given: user sets a channel-level notification override

then.description: Channel-specific preference stored on the membership record

channel_preference_overridden.result: That channel uses the override instead of the user's global default

given: user enables DND (optionally with an end time)

then.description: Current status saved so it can be restored on DND expiry
If timed, end time truncated to next minute boundary and stored

dnd_activated.result: Push notifications suppressed until DND is manually disabled or expires

given: user's dnd_end_time is in the past
dnd_expired.result: DND lifted; notifications resume according to user preferences

given: incoming push notification triggered for a user
push_suppressed_by_dnd.result: Push notification not delivered; notification reason recorded as user status

errors.message: The selected notification level is not valid.
The specified channel was not found.

events.name: notification_prefs.updated
events.description: User updated their global notification preferences

events.name: notification.suppressed
events.description: A notification was suppressed due to user preference or DND status

related.reason: Email notification preferences are part of the same preference set
DND status directly suppresses push notifications
Channel-level muting (mark_unread = mention) is implemented via the same notification preference mechanism

goals.description: User-controlled notification preference system with per-channel overrides and a Do-Not-Disturb mode that suppresses all notifications for a configurable period with automatic expiry.

success_metrics.measurement: Successful operations divided by total attempts
Failed operations divided by total attempts

tradeoffs.reason: notifications must reach recipients even if delayed
